title: Choosing Employment Weights: OEWS versus PUMS
text: claim national labor market, OEWS natural default. claim subgroup, geography, survey-defined population, build PUMS weight panel state cell explicitly.
code: comparison <- tibble::tibble(   population = c("OEWS national", "PUMS-style F", "PUMS-style M"),   aggregate = c(oews_result$aggregate, pums_f$aggregate, pums_m$aggregate),   coverage = c(     oews_result$employment_coverage_share,     pums_f$employment_coverage_share,     pums_m$employment_coverage_share   ) )  comparison |>   onet_kable() score_span <- range(comparison$aggregate) pad <- max(0.05, diff(score_span))  ggplot2::ggplot(comparison, ggplot2::aes(   x = aggregate,   y = stats::reorder(population, aggregate) )) +   ggplot2::geom_point(color = onet2r_colors[["teal"]], size = 4.5) +   ggplot2::geom_text(     ggplot2::aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", aggregate)),     vjust = -1.2,     size = 3.6,     color = onet2r_colors[["ink"]]   ) +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(     xlim = c(score_span[1] - pad, score_span[2] + pad)   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Same score, different population",     subtitle = paste(       "Each weight source implies a different population.",       "Here the aggregate shifts only slightly."     ),     x = "Employment-weighted aggregate score",     y = NULL   ) +   onet2r_theme()

title: Getting Started with onet2r

code: panel <- onet_panel(   "Abilities",   versions = c("30.2", "30.3"),   scale = "IM",   archives = c(`30.2` = archive_302, `30.3` = archive_303),   release_dates = c(`30.2` = "2026-02-01", `30.3` = "2026-05-01") )  changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(   panel,   bridge = onet_crosswalk_bridge("2019", "2019") )  changes |>   select(     to_soc_code,     element_name,     from_value,     to_value,     value_change,     change_type,     safely_comparable   ) |>   arrange(desc(abs(value_change))) |>   head(8) |>   onet_kable() plot_data <- changes |>   count(change_type, name = "rows") |>   arrange(desc(rows))  ggplot2::ggplot(plot_data, ggplot2::aes(   x = stats::reorder(change_type, rows),   y = rows,   fill = change_type )) +   ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +   ggplot2::coord_flip() +   onet2r_discrete_fill() +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "What Kind of Change Was Observed?",     subtitle = "Interpret the classification before the numeric change.",     x = NULL,     y = "Rows"   ) +   onet2r_theme()

title: How to Fool Yourself with O*NET over Time
text: making historical claim, ask: value change? source date change? occupation cross taxonomy seam? either row transition data suppressed? package gives fields caveats get lost.
code: cross_panel <- onet_panel(   "Abilities",   versions = c("24.3", "25.1"),   scale = "IM",   archives = c(     `24.3` = file.path(archive_base, "db_24_3_text"),     `25.1` = file.path(archive_base, "db_25_1_text")   ),   release_dates = c(`24.3` = "2020-08-01", `25.1` = "2020-11-01") )  bridge <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "2010",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_onet_soc_code = c("15-1132.00", "15-1132.00", "29-1141.00"),   to_onet_soc_code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1253.00", "29-1141.00"),   map_type = c("split", "split", "one_to_one"),   crosswalk_weight = c(0.5, 0.5, 1) )  seam <- onet_panel_reconcile(cross_panel, bridge)  seam |>   select(     from_onet_soc_code,     to_onet_soc_code,     element_name,     change_type,     transition_data,     crosswalk_uncertain,     safely_comparable   ) |>   onet_kable() safe_counts <- seam |>   mutate(comparability = if_else(safely_comparable, "Safe", "Not safe")) |>   count(comparability, name = "rows")  ggplot2::ggplot(safe_counts, ggplot2::aes(   x = comparability,   y = rows,   fill = comparability )) +   ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +   ggplot2::coord_flip() +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Safe" = onet2r_colors[["teal"]], "Not safe" = onet2r_colors[["rose"]])   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Taxonomy Seams Reduce Safe Comparisons",     subtitle = "A split occupation carries extra uncertainty even when codes can be bridged.",     x = NULL,     y = "Rows"   ) +   onet2r_theme()

title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: -vintage comparisons easy case. next example uses bundled 2010-vintage 2019-vintage fixtures. bridge intentionally tiny, shows real problem: 2010 occupation can split one 2019 occupation.
code: cross_archives <- c(   `24.3` = example_archive("24.3"),   `25.1` = example_archive("25.1") ) cross_dates <- c(`24.3` = "2020-08-01", `25.1` = "2020-11-01")  cross_panel <- onet_panel(   "Abilities",   versions = c("24.3", "25.1"),   scale = "IM",   archives = cross_archives,   release_dates = cross_dates )  bridge_2010_2019 <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "2010",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_onet_soc_code = c("15-1132.00", "15-1132.00", "29-1141.00"),   to_onet_soc_code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1253.00", "29-1141.00"),   map_type = c("split", "split", "one_to_one"),   crosswalk_weight = c(0.5, 0.5, 1) )  cross_changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(cross_panel, bridge_2010_2019)  cross_changes |>   select(     from_onet_soc_code,     to_onet_soc_code,     element_name,     change_type,     crosswalk_uncertain,     transition_data,     safely_comparable   ) |>   onet_kable() plot_changes <- cross_changes |>   filter(!is.na(from_value), !is.na(to_value)) |>   mutate(     comparison = paste(to_onet_soc_code, element_name, sep = ": "),     comparability = if_else(safely_comparable, "Safe", "Not safe")   )  ggplot2::ggplot(plot_changes, ggplot2::aes(y = comparison)) +   ggplot2::geom_segment(     ggplot2::aes(       x = from_value,       xend = to_value,       yend = comparison,       color = comparability     ),     linewidth = 1.1   ) +   ggplot2::geom_point(     ggplot2::aes(x = from_value),     color = onet2r_colors[["slate"]],     size = 2.8   ) +   ggplot2::geom_point(     ggplot2::aes(x = to_value, color = comparability),     size = 3.2   ) +   onet2r_discrete_color(name = "Comparability") +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "A Cross-Vintage Difference Is Not Automatically Comparable",     subtitle = "Start and end points come from the bundled archive fixtures.",     x = "Importance rating",     y = NULL   ) +   onet2r_theme()

text: comparability flag comes onet_panel_reconcile(), hand-labeled example table. Rows cross split transition data kept accounting get treated clean within-occupation change.  component rows total_change sum total change.
code: archive_base <- system.file("extdata", "onet-mini", package = "onet2r") cross_panel <- onet_panel(   "Abilities",   versions = c("24.3", "25.1"),   scale = "IM",   archives = c(     `24.3` = file.path(archive_base, "db_24_3_text"),     `25.1` = file.path(archive_base, "db_25_1_text")   ),   release_dates = c(`24.3` = "2020-08-01", `25.1` = "2020-11-01") )  bridge <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "2010",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_onet_soc_code = c("15-1132.00", "15-1132.00", "29-1141.00"),   to_onet_soc_code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1253.00", "29-1141.00"),   map_type = c("split", "split", "one_to_one"),   crosswalk_weight = c(0.5, 0.5, 1) )  ability_changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(cross_panel, bridge) score_changes <- ability_changes |>   filter(element_id == "1.A.1.a.1", !is.na(from_value), !is.na(to_value)) |>   mutate(reference_soc_code = sub("\\.\\d{2}$", "", to_onet_soc_code))  from_scores <- score_changes |>   select(     reference_soc_code,     measure_score = from_value,     safely_comparable   ) to_scores <- score_changes |>   select(     reference_soc_code,     measure_score = to_value,     safely_comparable   )  from_weights <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "15-1253", "29-1141"),   employment = c(120, 80, 200) ) to_weights <- tibble::tibble(   reference_soc_code = c("15-1252", "15-1253", "29-1141"),   employment = c(170, 110, 160) )  from_scores |>   onet_kable() to_scores |>   onet_kable() decomp <- onet_decompose_change(   from_scores,   to_scores,   from_weights,   to_weights )  decomp |>   select(component, value) |>   onet_kable() onet_coverage(decomp) |>   onet_kable() components <- decomp |>   filter(component != "total_change") |>   mutate(     label = tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", component)),     label = factor(label, levels = rev(label)),     direction = if_else(value >= 0, "Raises the aggregate", "Lowers the aggregate")   )  ggplot2::ggplot(components, ggplot2::aes(x = value, y = label, fill = direction)) +   ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.62) +   ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = onet2r_colors[["slate"]]) +   ggplot2::geom_text(     ggplot2::aes(       label = sprintf("%+.3f", value),       hjust = if_else(value >= 0, -0.15, 1.15)     ),     size = 3.5,     color = onet2r_colors[["ink"]]   ) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       "Raises the aggregate" = onet2r_colors[["teal"]],       "Lowers the aggregate" = onet2r_colors[["rose"]]     )   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(     expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0.16, 0.16))   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "Where did the aggregate change come from?",     subtitle = "Each piece's contribution to the total change.",     x = "Contribution to total change",     y = NULL,     fill = NULL   ) +   onet2r_theme() decomp |>   summarise(     component_sum = sum(value[component != "total_change"]),     total_change = value[component == "total_change"],     difference = component_sum - total_change   ) |>   onet_kable()

code: panel <- tibble::tibble(   release_version = c("29.0", "30.0", "29.0", "30.0"),   release_date = as.Date(c("2024-08-01", "2025-08-01", "2024-08-01", "2025-08-01")),   soc_vintage = rep("2019", 4),   domain = "Abilities",   onet_soc_code = rep("11-1011.00", 4),   soc_code = rep("11-1011", 4),   element_id = c("1.A.1.a.1", "1.A.1.a.1", "1.A.1.a.2", "1.A.1.a.2"),   element_name = c(     "Oral Comprehension", "Oral Comprehension",     "Written Comprehension", "Written Comprehension"   ),   scale_id = rep("IM", 4),   data_value = c(4.5, 4.7, 4.2, 4.2),   source_date = as.Date(c("2023-08-01", "2024-08-01", "2023-08-01", "2023-08-01")),   domain_source = rep("Analyst", 4) ) bridge <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "2019",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_soc_code = "11-1011",   to_soc_code = "11-1011",   map_type = "one_to_one",   crosswalk_weight = 1 ) onet_panel_reconcile(panel, bridge) #> # A tibble: 2 × 35 #>   from_release to_release from_release_date to_release_date from_onet_soc_code #>   <chr>        <chr>      <date>            <date>          <chr>              #> 1 29.0         30.0       2024-08-01        2025-08-01      11-1011.00         #> 2 29.0         30.0       2024-08-01        2025-08-01      11-1011.00         #> # ℹ 30 more variables: to_onet_soc_code <chr>, from_soc_code <chr>, #> #   to_soc_code <chr>, soc_vintage_from <chr>, soc_vintage_to <chr>, #> #   domain <chr>, element_id <chr>, element_name <chr>, scale_id <chr>, #> #   from_value <dbl>, to_value <dbl>, value_change <dbl>, #> #   value_percent_change <dbl>, from_source_date <date>, to_source_date <date>, #> #   from_domain_source <chr>, to_domain_source <chr>, #> #   from_recommend_suppress <chr>, to_recommend_suppress <chr>, …

title: onet2r 0.4.1
text: Rebuilt README article figures shared ggplot2 style pkgdown examples render readable, dark-mode-safe output instead base graphics. Replaced broad console table dumps README vignettes bounded knitr::kable() tables actual onet2r function results. Expanded longitudinal archive, OEWS, sensitivity, decomposition articles practical cross-vintage examples using packaged fixtures. Added pkgdown callouts, branded favicon colors, citation metadata, lifecycle badge documentation, release notes v0.4.1 polish pass. Strengthened fixture tests task rating scale handling multi-cell aggregation guards.

title: onet2r 0.4.0
text: onet_coverage() onet_provenance() expose coverage provenance metadata durable accessors instead requiring users inspect attributes. onet_decompose_change() now returns coverage metadata accessor-readable list-column. onet_join_oews(), onet_pums_employment_weights(), onet_weighted_summary() now emit lifecycle guidance toward vintage-aware onet_weight_panel_oews(), onet_weight_panel_pums(), onet_measure_aggregate() workflow. onet_measure_aggregate() now requires single weight year one optional cell, explicit year cell filters multi-period grouped panels. onet_measure_sensitivity() runs user-supplied measure across alternative bridges, weight panels, task-handling choices, reports baseline movement provenance. Added cross-vintage task-rating fixtures tests articles can demonstrate 2010--2019 O*NET-SOC behavior without network access.

title: onet2r 0.3.0
text: Added CI, README.Rmd, reproducible package logo source cleaner release maintenance. onet_archive_read() now preserves task DWA native fields reading O*NET Task Statements, Task Ratings, Tasks DWAs, 30.3 GWAs IWAs DWAs file. onet_crosswalk_bridge() now keeps 8-digit O*NET-SOC detail codes native bridge grain includes derived 6-digit SOC columns employment joins. onet_decompose_change() decomposes aggregate change within, , interaction, unclassifiable components sum--total check. onet_measure(), onet_task_to_occupation(), onet_measure_aggregate(), onet_robustness_diagnostic() add bring---measure validation, task--occupation aggregation, employment-weighted aggregation, provenance, plumbing-sensitivity checks. onet_panel_reconcile() now classifies transition rows, suppressed rows, new rows, dropped rows explicitly instead counting ordinary matched updates. onet_reference_soc_resolve(), onet_weight_panel_oews(), onet_weight_panel_pums() create reference-SOC mappings normalized employment-weight panels OEWS PUMS workflows.

title: onet2r 0.2.0
text: Added longitudinal O*NET background article verified reference file help users understand archive panels, taxonomy changes, comparability limits interpreting release--release changes. onet_archive_download() downloads text-format O*NET database archives release version reproducible local analysis. onet_archive_read() reads descriptor tables downloaded archives normalized long panel schema now accepts local ZIP files extracted archive directories offline workflows. onet_change_summary() summarizes reconciled O*NET changes overall SOC job family. onet_crosswalk_bridge() builds SOC taxonomy bridges across supported O*NET vintages labels one--one, split, merge mappings. onet_panel() assembles descriptor tables across multiple O*NET releases longitudinal analysis can use local archive paths explicit release dates. onet_panel_reconcile() classifies release--release changes real updates, stable resampling, stale carryforwards, recode/recalculation flags. onet_releases() lists O*NET database releases, archive URLs, dictionary URLs, release dates, inferred SOC taxonomy vintages.

title: onet2r 0.1.0
text: onet_cache_use(), onet_cache_clear(), onet_rate_limit() add optional local response caching request spacing repeated bulk O*NET API workflows. onet_join_oews() joins O*NET occupation tibbles national BLS Occupational Employment Wage Statistics (OEWS) employment wage estimates. onet_oews(), onet_oews_national(), onet_oews_state(), onet_oews_metro(), onet_oews_industry() download parse BLS OEWS estimates SOC-level wage employment context. onet_pums_employment_weights() converts ACS/CPS-style PUMS occupation records SOC-level employment weights O*NET joins. onet_weighted_summary() computes employment- wage-weighted summaries O*NET task, skill, ability, work-activity rows. Initial development release O*NET authentication, occupation search, occupation detail helpers, database table access, pagination, military crosswalks, taxonomy mapping.
